text: Every banana emits a tiny bit of radiation — and that is completely normal. What you’re tasting is potassium-40, a naturally occurring isotope. Yes, it's radioactive. But don’t worry! The levels are tiny — so tiny, they're harmless. In fact, you’d need to eat about 10,000 bananas in one sitting to feel any effect! That’s why scientists developed the 'banana equivalent dose' — a fun way to measure radiation! Remember, everything around us has some level of radioactivity. It’s perfectly natural. Next time you enjoy a banana, appreciate its unique quirks! Like and follow for more weird science!
